candidates.name: Spencer Cox
candidates.notes: 18th Governor of Utah (2021-). Lifelong Latter-day Saint — served LDS mission in Mexico, was bishop in his ward. NRA A rating. Moderate Republican. Signed abortion trigger ban but more moderate than legislature. Left Twitter/X calling it 'transformational' for health. Known for civility and disagree-better campaigns. Signed bill banning transgender youth medical care.

profile.education: Snow College (AS); Utah State University (BA, Political Science); Washington and Lee University School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Attorney and rural leader; served as Fairview mayor, Sanpete County commissioner, and Utah lieutenant governor; became 18th governor in 2021.

candidates.name: Mike Lee
candidates.notes: Senior Senator from Utah (2011-). Latter-day Saint — served LDS mission in Texas Rio Grande Valley. Heritage Action lifetime 95% — one of highest in Senate. NRA A+. Constitutional originalist. Strong Tea Party/liberty caucus member. Co-sponsor of multiple pro-life bills. Compared Trump to Book of Mormon hero Captain Moroni (controversial). Most conservative member of Utah delegation.

profile.education: Brigham Young University (BA Political Science), BYU J. Reuben Clark Law School (JD)
profile.background: Constitutional lawyer and former federal prosecutor. Son of former Solicitor General Rex Lee. Clerked for Justice Samuel Alito on Third Circuit. In Senate since 2011.

candidates.name: John Curtis
candidates.notes: Junior Senator from Utah (2025-). Latter-day Saint — served LDS mission in Taiwan. Heritage Action lifetime 89% (session 94%). NRA A. Descendant of Brigham Young. Former Mayor of Provo. Considered moderate Republican despite high Heritage score. Co-sponsored Fairness for All Act balancing religious liberty with LGBTQ protections. Replaced Mitt Romney's seat.

profile.background: Former Mayor of Provo and U.S. House member. Served LDS mission in Taiwan, fluent in Mandarin. Worked at OC Tanner and Citizen Watch Company before entering politics.
